description: Small finches  Short bill   Red cap, much less extensive than the read on a housefinch  Pinkish wash on breast  Notably white below, with streaking only on sides of breast  Dark chin patchBehaviors: On two different occasions, redpolls were seen eating nyjer seed at a finch feeder outside our kitchen window.   (The feeder is positioned behind a broad frame between the two halves of the window, which prevents kitchen users from startling birds but, when combined with the wavy glass of a century-old house, makes photography almost impossible.)  We only saw two at one time and one at another time, but we are rarely home in the daylight hours at that time of year. That feeder is very popular and birds cycle through it rapidly, so I have no idea how many there actually were. They seemed to be staging in a large, dense spruce tree with house finches and a few goldfinches. There is no really logical redpoll habitat nearby.Call: Not heardPlumage: Winter
